NKJV.1: A Contemplation of Asaph. O God, why have You cast us off forever? Why does Your anger smoke against the sheep of Your pasture?
NKJV.2: Remember Your congregation, which You have purchased of old, The tribe of Your inheritance, which You have redeemed— This Mount Zion where You have dwelt.
NKJV.3: Lift up Your feet to the perpetual desolations. The enemy has damaged everything in the sanctuary.
NKJV.4: Your enemies roar in the midst of Your meeting place; They set up their banners for signs.
NKJV.5: They seem like men who lift up Axes among the thick trees.
NKJV.6: And now they break down its carved work, all at once, With axes and hammers.
NKJV.7: They have set fire to Your sanctuary; They have defiled the dwelling place of Your name to the ground.
NKJV.8: They said in their hearts, “Let us destroy them altogether.” They have burned up all the meeting places of God in the land.
NKJV.9: We do not see our signs; There is no longer any prophet; Nor is there any among us who knows how long.
NKJV.10: O God, how long will the adversary reproach? Will the enemy blaspheme Your name forever?
NKJV.11: Why do You withdraw Your hand, even Your right hand? Take it out of Your bosom and destroy them.
NKJV.12: For God is my King from of old, Working salvation in the midst of the earth.
NKJV.13: You divided the sea by Your strength; You broke the heads of the sea serpents in the waters.
NKJV.14: You broke the heads of Leviathan in pieces, And gave him as food to the people inhabiting the wilderness.
NKJV.15: You broke open the fountain and the flood;  You dried up mighty rivers.
NKJV.16: The day is Yours, the night also is Yours; You have prepared the light and the sun.
NKJV.17: You have set all the borders of the earth;  You have made summer and winter.
NKJV.18: Remember this, that the enemy has reproached, O LORD, And that a foolish people has blasphemed Your name.
NKJV.19: Oh, do not deliver the life of Your turtledove to the wild beast! Do not forget the life of Your poor forever.
NKJV.20: Have respect to the covenant; For the dark places of the earth are full of the haunts of cruelty.
NKJV.21: Oh, do not let the oppressed return ashamed! Let the poor and needy praise Your name.
NKJV.22: Arise, O God, plead Your own cause; Remember how the foolish man reproaches You daily.
NKJV.23: Do not forget the voice of Your enemies; The tumult of those who rise up against You increases continually.

KJV.1: Maschil<S>4905</S> of Asaph<S>623</S>. O God<S>430</S>, why hast thou cast us off<S>2186</S> for ever<S>5331</S>? why doth thine anger<S>639</S> smoke<S>6225</S> against the sheep<S>6629</S> of thy pasture<S>4830</S>? <sup>Maschil: or, A Psalm for Asaph to give instruction</sup>
KJV.2: Remember<S>2142</S> thy congregation<S>5712</S>, which thou hast purchased<S>7069</S> of old<S>6924</S>; the rod<S>7626</S> of thine inheritance<S>5159</S>, which thou hast redeemed<S>1350</S>; this mount<S>2022</S> Zion<S>6726</S>, wherein thou hast dwelt<S>7931</S>. <sup>rod: or, tribe</sup>
KJV.3: Lift up<S>7311</S> thy feet<S>6471</S> unto the perpetual<S>5331</S> desolations<S>4876</S>; even all that the enemy<S>341</S> hath done wickedly<S>7489</S> in the sanctuary<S>6944</S>.
KJV.4: Thine enemies<S>6887</S> roar<S>7580</S> in the midst<S>7130</S> of thy congregations<S>4150</S>; they set up<S>7760</S> their ensigns<S>226</S> for signs<S>226</S>.
KJV.5: A man was famous<S>3045</S> according as he had lifted up<S>935</S> <S>4605</S> axes<S>7134</S> upon the thick<S>5442</S> trees<S>6086</S>.
KJV.6: But now they break down<S>1986</S> the carved work<S>6603</S> thereof at once<S>3162</S> with axes<S>3781</S> and hammers<S>3597</S>.
KJV.7: They have cast<S>7971</S> fire<S>784</S> into thy sanctuary<S>4720</S>, they have defiled<S>2490</S> by casting down the dwelling place<S>4908</S> of thy name<S>8034</S> to the ground<S>776</S>. <sup>They have cast: Heb. They have sent thy sanctuary unto the fire</sup>
KJV.8: They said<S>559</S> in their hearts<S>3820</S>, Let us destroy<S>3238</S> them together<S>3162</S>: they have burned up<S>8313</S> all the synagogues<S>4150</S> of God<S>410</S> in the land<S>776</S>. <sup>destroy: Heb. break</sup>
KJV.9: We see<S>7200</S> not our signs<S>226</S>: there is no more any prophet<S>5030</S>: neither is there among us any that knoweth<S>3045</S> how long<S>5704</S>.
KJV.10: O God<S>430</S>, how long shall the adversary<S>6862</S> reproach<S>2778</S>? shall the enemy<S>341</S> blaspheme<S>5006</S> thy name<S>8034</S> for ever<S>5331</S>?
KJV.11: Why withdrawest<S>7725</S> thou thy hand<S>3027</S>, even thy right hand<S>3225</S>? pluck<S>3615</S> it out of<S>7130</S> thy bosom<S>2436</S> <S>2436</S>.
KJV.12: For God<S>430</S> is my King<S>4428</S> of old<S>6924</S>, working<S>6466</S> salvation<S>3444</S> in the midst<S>7130</S> of the earth<S>776</S>.
KJV.13: Thou didst divide<S>6565</S> the sea<S>3220</S> by thy strength<S>5797</S>: thou brakest<S>7665</S> the heads<S>7218</S> of the dragons<S>8577</S> in the waters<S>4325</S>. <sup>divide: Heb. break</sup> <sup>dragons: or, whales</sup>
KJV.14: Thou brakest<S>7533</S> the heads<S>7218</S> of leviathan<S>3882</S> in pieces, and gavest<S>5414</S> him to be meat<S>3978</S> to the people<S>5971</S> inhabiting the wilderness<S>6728</S>.
KJV.15: Thou didst cleave<S>1234</S> the fountain<S>4599</S> and the flood<S>5158</S>: thou driedst up<S>3001</S> mighty<S>386</S> rivers<S>5104</S>. <sup>mighty: Heb. rivers of strength</sup>
KJV.16: The day<S>3117</S> is thine, the night<S>3915</S> also is thine: thou hast prepared<S>3559</S> the light<S>3974</S> and the sun<S>8121</S>.
KJV.17: Thou hast set<S>5324</S> all the borders<S>1367</S> of the earth<S>776</S>: thou hast made<S>3335</S> summer<S>7019</S> and winter<S>2779</S>. <sup>made: Heb. made them</sup>
KJV.18: Remember<S>2142</S> this, that the enemy<S>341</S> hath reproached<S>2778</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, and that the foolish<S>5036</S> people<S>5971</S> have blasphemed<S>5006</S> thy name<S>8034</S>.
KJV.19: O deliver<S>5414</S> not the soul<S>5315</S> of thy turtledove<S>8449</S> unto the multitude<S>2416</S> of the wicked: forget<S>7911</S> not the congregation<S>2416</S> of thy poor<S>6041</S> for ever<S>5331</S>.
KJV.20: Have respect<S>5027</S> unto the covenant<S>1285</S>: for the dark<S>4285</S> places of the earth<S>776</S> are full<S>4390</S> of the habitations<S>4999</S> of cruelty<S>2555</S>.
KJV.21: O let not the oppressed<S>1790</S> return<S>7725</S> ashamed<S>3637</S>: let the poor<S>6041</S> and needy<S>34</S> praise<S>1984</S> thy name<S>8034</S>.
KJV.22: Arise<S>6965</S>, O God<S>430</S>, plead<S>7378</S> thine own cause<S>7379</S>: remember<S>2142</S> how the foolish man<S>5036</S> reproacheth<S>2781</S> thee daily<S>3117</S>.
KJV.23: Forget<S>7911</S> not the voice<S>6963</S> of thine enemies<S>6887</S>: the tumult<S>7588</S> of those that rise up<S>6965</S> against thee increaseth<S>5927</S> continually<S>8548</S>. <sup>increaseth: Heb. ascendeth</sup>

ESV.1: O God, why do you cast us off forever? Why does your anger smoke against the sheep of your pasture?
ESV.2: Remember your congregation, which you have purchased of old, which you have redeemed to be the tribe of your heritage! Remember Mount Zion, where you have dwelt.
ESV.3: Direct your steps to the perpetual ruins; the enemy has destroyed everything in the sanctuary!
ESV.4: Your foes have roared in the midst of your meeting place; they set up their own signs for signs.
ESV.5: They were like those who swing axes in a forest of trees.
ESV.6: And all its carved wood they broke down with hatchets and hammers.
ESV.7: They set your sanctuary on fire; they profaned the dwelling place of your name, bringing it down to the ground.
ESV.8: They said to themselves, “We will utterly subdue them”; they burned all the meeting places of God in the land.
ESV.9: We do not see our signs; there is no longer any prophet, and there is none among us who knows how long.
ESV.10: How long, O God, is the foe to scoff? Is the enemy to revile your name forever?
ESV.11: Why do you hold back your hand, your right hand? Take it from the fold of your garment  and destroy them!
ESV.12: Yet God my King is from of old, working salvation in the midst of the earth.
ESV.13: You divided the sea by your might; you broke the heads of the sea monsters  on the waters.
ESV.14: You crushed the heads of Leviathan; you gave him as food for the creatures of the wilderness.
ESV.15: You split open springs and brooks; you dried up ever-flowing streams.
ESV.16: Yours is the day, yours also the night; you have established the heavenly lights and the sun.
ESV.17: You have fixed all the boundaries of the earth; you have made summer and winter.
ESV.18: Remember this, O LORD, how the enemy scoffs, and a foolish people reviles your name.
ESV.19: Do not deliver the soul of your dove to the wild beasts; do not forget the life of your poor forever.
ESV.20: Have regard for the covenant, for the dark places of the land are full of the habitations of violence.
ESV.21: Let not the downtrodden turn back in shame; let the poor and needy praise your name.
ESV.22: Arise, O God, defend your cause; remember how the foolish scoff at you all the day!
ESV.23: Do not forget the clamor of your foes, the uproar of those who rise against you, which goes up continually!

NASB.1: A Maskil of Asaph. O God, why have You rejected [us] forever? Why does Your anger smoke against the sheep of Your pasture?
NASB.2: Remember Your congregation, which You have purchased of old, Which You have redeemed to be the tribe of Your inheritance; [And] this Mount Zion, where You have dwelt.
NASB.3: Turn Your footsteps toward the perpetual ruins; The enemy has damaged everything within the sanctuary.
NASB.4: Your adversaries have roared in the midst of Your meeting place; They have set up their own standards for signs.
NASB.5: It seems as if one had lifted up [His] axe in a forest of trees.
NASB.6: And now all its carved work They smash with hatchet and hammers.
NASB.7: They have burned Your sanctuary to the ground; They have defiled the dwelling place of Your name.
NASB.8: They said in their heart, 'Let us completely subdue them.' They have burned all the meeting places of God in the land.
NASB.9: We do not see our signs; There is no longer any prophet, Nor is there any among us who knows how long.
NASB.10: How long, O God, will the adversary revile, [And] the enemy spurn Your name forever?
NASB.11: Why do You withdraw Your hand, even Your right hand? From within Your bosom, destroy [them]!
NASB.12: Yet God is my king from of old, Who works deeds of deliverance in the midst of the earth.
NASB.13: You divided the sea by Your strength; You broke the heads of the sea monsters in the waters.
NASB.14: You crushed the heads of Leviathan; You gave him as food for the creatures of the wilderness.
NASB.15: You broke open springs and torrents; You dried up ever-flowing streams.
NASB.16: Yours is the day, Yours also is the night; You have prepared the light and the sun.
NASB.17: You have established all the boundaries of the earth; You have made summer and winter.
NASB.18: Remember this, O LORD, that the enemy has reviled, And a foolish people has spurned Your name.
NASB.19: Do not deliver the soul of Your turtledove to the wild beast; Do not forget the life of Your afflicted forever.
NASB.20: Consider the covenant; For the dark places of the land are full of the habitations of violence.
NASB.21: Let not the oppressed return dishonored; Let the afflicted and needy praise Your name.
NASB.22: Arise, O God, [and] plead Your own cause; Remember how the foolish man reproaches You all day long.
NASB.23: Do not forget the voice of Your adversaries, The uproar of those who rise against You which ascends continually.

NLT.1:
O God, why have you rejected us so long?
Why is your anger so intense against the sheep of your own pasture?
NLT.2:
Remember that we are the people you chose long ago,
the tribe you redeemed as your own special possession!
And remember Jerusalem, your home here on earth.
NLT.3:
Walk through the awful ruins of the city;
see how the enemy has destroyed your sanctuary.
NLT.4:
There your enemies shouted their victorious battle cries;
there they set up their battle standards.
NLT.5:
They swung their axes
like woodcutters in a forest.
NLT.6:
With axes and picks,
they smashed the carved paneling.
NLT.7:
They burned your sanctuary to the ground.
They defiled the place that bears your name.
NLT.8:
Then they thought, “Let’s destroy everything!”
So they burned down all the places where God was worshiped.
NLT.9:
We no longer see your miraculous signs.
All the prophets are gone,
and no one can tell us when it will end.
NLT.10:
How long, O God, will you allow our enemies to insult you?
Will you let them dishonor your name forever?
NLT.11:
Why do you hold back your strong right hand?
Unleash your powerful fist and destroy them.
NLT.12:
You, O God, are my king from ages past,
bringing salvation to the earth.
NLT.13:
You split the sea by your strength
and smashed the heads of the sea monsters.
NLT.14:
You crushed the heads of Leviathan
and let the desert animals eat him.
NLT.15:
You caused the springs and streams to gush forth,
and you dried up rivers that never run dry.
NLT.16:
Both day and night belong to you;
you made the starlight and the sun.
NLT.17:
You set the boundaries of the earth,
and you made both summer and winter.
NLT.18:
See how these enemies insult you, LORD.
A foolish nation has dishonored your name.
NLT.19:
Don’t let these wild beasts destroy your turtledoves.
Don’t forget your suffering people forever.
NLT.20:
Remember your covenant promises,
for the land is full of darkness and violence!
NLT.21:
Don’t let the downtrodden be humiliated again.
Instead, let the poor and needy praise your name.
NLT.22:
Arise, O God, and defend your cause.
Remember how these fools insult you all day long.
NLT.23:
Don’t overlook what your enemies have said
or their growing uproar.

WEB.1: A contemplation by Asaph. God, why have you rejected us forever? Why does your anger smolder against the sheep of your pasture?
WEB.2: Remember your congregation, which you purchased of old, which you have redeemed to be the tribe of your inheritance; Mount Zion, in which you have lived.
WEB.3: Lift up your feet to the perpetual ruins, all the evil that the enemy has done in the sanctuary.
WEB.4: Your adversaries have roared in the middle of your assembly. They have set up their standards as signs.
WEB.5: They behaved like men wielding axes, cutting through a thicket of trees.
WEB.6: Now they break all its carved work down with hatchet and hammers.
WEB.7: They have burned your sanctuary to the ground. They have profaned the dwelling place of your Name.
WEB.8: They said in their heart, “We will crush them completely.” They have burned up all the places in the land where God was worshiped.
WEB.9: We see no miraculous signs. There is no longer any prophet, neither is there among us anyone who knows how long.
WEB.10: How long, God, shall the adversary reproach? Shall the enemy blaspheme your name forever?
WEB.11: Why do you draw back your hand, even your right hand? Take it out of your pocket and consume them!
WEB.12: Yet God is my King of old, working salvation throughout the earth.
WEB.13: You divided the sea by your strength. You broke the heads of the sea monsters in the waters.
WEB.14: You broke the heads of Leviathan in pieces. You gave him as food to people and desert creatures.
WEB.15: You opened up spring and stream. You dried up mighty rivers.
WEB.16: The day is yours, the night is also yours. You have prepared the light and the sun.
WEB.17: You have set all the boundaries of the earth. You have made summer and winter.
WEB.18: Remember this, that the enemy has mocked you, Yahweh. Foolish people have blasphemed your name.
WEB.19: Don’t deliver the soul of your dove to wild beasts. Don’t forget the life of your poor forever.
WEB.20: Honor your covenant, for haunts of violence fill the dark places of the earth.
WEB.21: Don’t let the oppressed return ashamed. Let the poor and needy praise your name.
WEB.22: Arise, God! Plead your own cause. Remember how the foolish man mocks you all day.
WEB.23: Don’t forget the voice of your adversaries. The tumult of those who rise up against you ascends continually.

AMP.1:
A skillful song, or a didactic or reflective poem, of Asaph. O God, why have You rejected us forever?
Why does Your anger smoke against the sheep of Your pasture?
AMP.2:
Remember Your congregation, which You have purchased of old,
Which You have redeemed to be the tribe of Your inheritance;
Remember Mount Zion, where You have dwelt.
AMP.3:
Turn your footsteps [quickly] toward the perpetual ruins;
The enemy has damaged everything within the sanctuary.
AMP.4:
In the midst of Your meeting place Your enemies have roared [with their battle cry];
They have set up their own emblems for signs [of victory].
AMP.5:
It seems as if one had lifted up
An axe in a forest of trees [to set a record of destruction].
AMP.6:
And now all the carved work [of the meeting place]
They smash with hatchets and hammers.
AMP.7:
They have burned Your sanctuary to the ground;
They have profaned the dwelling place of Your name.
AMP.8:
They said in their heart, "Let us completely subdue them."
They have burned all the meeting places of God in the land.
AMP.9:
We do not see our symbols;
There is no longer any prophet [to guide us],
Nor does any among us know for how long.
AMP.10:
O God, how long will the adversary scoff?
Is the enemy to revile Your name forever?
AMP.11:
Why do You withdraw Your hand, even Your right hand [from judging the enemy]?
Remove Your hand from Your chest, destroy them!
AMP.12:
Yet God is my King of old,
Working salvation in the midst of the earth.
AMP.13:
You divided the [Red] Sea by Your strength;
You broke the heads of the sea monsters in the waters.
AMP.14:
You crushed the heads of Leviathan (Egypt);
You gave him as food for the creatures of the wilderness.
AMP.15:
You broke open fountains and streams;
You dried up ever-flowing rivers.
AMP.16:
The day is Yours, the night also is Yours;
You have established and prepared the [heavenly] light and the sun.
AMP.17:
You have defined and established all the borders of the earth [the divisions of land and sea and of the nations];
You have made summer and winter.
AMP.18:
Remember this, O LORD, the enemy has scoffed,
And a foolish and impious people has spurned Your name.
AMP.19:
Oh, do not hand over the soul of your turtledove to the wild beast;
Do not forget the life of Your afflicted forever.
AMP.20:
Consider the covenant [You made with Abraham],
For the dark places of the land are full of the habitations of violence.
AMP.21:
Let not the oppressed return dishonored;
Let the afflicted and needy praise Your name.
AMP.22:
Arise, O God, plead Your own cause;
Remember how the foolish man scoffs at You all day long.
AMP.23:
Do not forget the [clamoring] voices of Your adversaries,
The uproar of those who rise against You, which ascends continually [to Your ears].

MSG.1:
An Asaph psalm
You walked off and left us, and never looked back.
God, how could you do that?
We're your very own sheep;
how can you stomp off in anger?
MSG.2:
Refresh your memory of us—you bought us a long time ago.
Your most precious tribe—you paid a good price for us!
Your very own Mount Zion—you actually lived here once!
MSG.3:
Come and visit the site of disaster,
see how they've wrecked the sanctuary.
MSG.4:
While your people were at worship, your enemies barged in,
brawling and scrawling graffiti.
MSG.5:
They set fire to the porch;
axes swinging, they chopped up the woodwork,
MSG.6:
Beat down the doors with sledgehammers,
then split them into kindling.
MSG.7:
They burned your holy place to the ground,
violated the place of worship.
MSG.8:
They said to themselves, "We'll wipe them all out,"
and burned down all the places of worship.
MSG.9:
There's not a sign or symbol of God in sight,
nor anyone to speak in his name,
no one who knows what's going on.
MSG.10:
How long, God, will barbarians blaspheme,
enemies curse and get by with it?
MSG.11:
Why don't you do something? How long are you going
to sit there with your hands folded in your lap?
MSG.12:
God is my King from the very start;
he works salvation in the womb of the earth.
MSG.13:
With one blow you split the sea in two,
you made mincemeat of the dragon Tannin.
MSG.14:
You lopped off the heads of Leviathan,
then served them up in a stew for the animals.
MSG.15:
With your finger you opened up springs and creeks,
and dried up the wild floodwaters.
MSG.16:
You own the day, you own the night;
you put stars and sun in place.
MSG.17:
You laid out the four corners of earth,
shaped the seasons of summer and winter.
MSG.18:
Mark and remember, God, all the enemy
taunts, each idiot desecration.
MSG.19:
Don't throw your lambs to the wolves;
after all we've been through, don't forget us.
MSG.20:
Remember your promises;
the city is in darkness, the countryside violent.
MSG.21:
Don't leave the victims to rot in the street;
make them a choir that sings your praises.
MSG.22:
On your feet, O God—
stand up for yourself!
Do you hear what they're saying about you,
all the vile obscenities?
MSG.23:
Don't tune out their malicious filth,
the brawling invective that never lets up.

NIV.1:
Psalm 74
A maskil of Asaph.
Why have you rejected us forever, O God?
Why does your anger smolder against the sheep of your pasture?
NIV.2:
Remember the people you purchased of old,
the tribe of your inheritance, whom you redeemed—
Mount Zion, where you dwelt.
NIV.3:
Turn your steps toward these everlasting ruins,
all this destruction the enemy has brought on the sanctuary.
NIV.4:
Your foes roared in the place where you met with us;
they set up their standards as signs.
NIV.5:
They behaved like men wielding axes
to cut through a thicket of trees.
NIV.6:
They smashed all the carved paneling
with their axes and hatchets.
NIV.7:
They burned your sanctuary to the ground;
they defiled the dwelling place of your Name.
NIV.8:
They said in their hearts, “We will crush them completely!”
They burned every place where God was worshiped in the land.
NIV.9:
We are given no miraculous signs;
no prophets are left,
and none of us knows how long this will be.
NIV.10:
How long will the enemy mock you, O God?
Will the foe revile your name forever?
NIV.11:
Why do you hold back your hand, your right hand?
Take it from the folds of your garment and destroy them!
NIV.12:
But you, O God, are my king from of old;
you bring salvation upon the earth.
NIV.13:
It was you who split open the sea by your power;
you broke the heads of the monster in the waters.
NIV.14:
It was you who crushed the heads of Leviathan
and gave him as food to the creatures of the desert.
NIV.15:
It was you who opened up springs and streams;
you dried up the ever flowing rivers.
NIV.16:
The day is yours, and yours also the night;
you established the sun and moon.
NIV.17:
It was you who set all the boundaries of the earth;
you made both summer and winter.
NIV.18:
Remember how the enemy has mocked you, O Lord,
how foolish people have reviled your name.
NIV.19:
Do not hand over the life of your dove to wild beasts;
do not forget the lives of your afflicted people forever.
NIV.20:
Have regard for your covenant,
because haunts of violence fill the dark places of the land.
NIV.21:
Do not let the oppressed retreat in disgrace;
may the poor and needy praise your name.
NIV.22:
Rise up, O God, and defend your cause;
remember how fools mock you all day long.
NIV.23:
Do not ignore the clamor of your adversaries,
the uproar of your enemies, which rises continually.

NRSVCE.1:
O God, why do you cast us off forever?
Why does your anger smoke against the sheep of your pasture?
NRSVCE.2:
Remember your congregation, which you acquired long ago,
which you redeemed to be the tribe of your heritage.
Remember Mount Zion, where you came to dwell.
NRSVCE.3:
Direct your steps to the perpetual ruins;
the enemy has destroyed everything in the sanctuary.
NRSVCE.4:
Your foes have roared within your holy place;
they set up their emblems there.
NRSVCE.5:
At the upper entrance they hacked
the wooden trellis with axes.
NRSVCE.6:
And then, with hatchets and hammers,
they smashed all its carved work.
NRSVCE.7:
They set your sanctuary on fire;
they desecrated the dwelling place of your name,
bringing it to the ground.
NRSVCE.8:
They said to themselves, “We will utterly subdue them”;
they burned all the meeting places of God in the land.
NRSVCE.9:
We do not see our emblems;
there is no longer any prophet,
and there is no one among us who knows how long.
NRSVCE.10:
How long, O God, is the foe to scoff?
Is the enemy to revile your name forever?
NRSVCE.11:
Why do you hold back your hand;
why do you keep your hand in your bosom?
NRSVCE.12:
Yet God my King is from of old,
working salvation in the earth.
NRSVCE.13:
You divided the sea by your might;
you broke the heads of the dragons in the waters.
NRSVCE.14:
You crushed the heads of Leviathan;
you gave him as food for the creatures of the wilderness.
NRSVCE.15:
You cut openings for springs and torrents;
you dried up ever-flowing streams.
NRSVCE.16:
Yours is the day, yours also the night;
you established the luminaries and the sun.
NRSVCE.17:
You have fixed all the bounds of the earth;
you made summer and winter.
NRSVCE.18:
Remember this, O LORD, how the enemy scoffs,
and an impious people reviles your name.
NRSVCE.19:
Do not deliver the soul of your dove to the wild animals;
do not forget the life of your poor forever.
NRSVCE.20:
Have regard for your covenant,
for the dark places of the land are full of the haunts of violence.
NRSVCE.21:
Do not let the downtrodden be put to shame;
let the poor and needy praise your name.
NRSVCE.22:
Rise up, O God, plead your cause;
remember how the impious scoff at you all day long.
NRSVCE.23:
Do not forget the clamor of your foes,
the uproar of your adversaries that goes up continually.

NET.1: Why, O God, have you permanently rejected us? Why does your anger burn against the sheep of your pasture?
NET.2: Remember your people whom you acquired in ancient times, whom you rescued so they could be your very own nation, as well as Mount Zion, where you dwell!
NET.3: Hurry and look at the permanent ruins, and all the damage the enemy has done to the temple!
NET.4: Your enemies roar in the middle of your sanctuary; they set up their battle flags.
NET.5: They invade like lumberjacks swinging their axes in a thick forest.
NET.6: And now they are tearing down all its engravings with axes and crowbars.
NET.7: They set your sanctuary on fire; they desecrate your dwelling place by knocking it to the ground.
NET.8: They say to themselves, “We will oppress all of them.” They burn down all the places where people worship God in the land.
NET.9: We do not see any signs of God’s presence; there are no longer any prophets and we have no one to tell us how long this will last.
NET.10: How long, O God, will the adversary hurl insults? Will the enemy blaspheme your name forever?
NET.11: Why do you remain inactive? Intervene and destroy him!
NET.12: But God has been my king from ancient times, performing acts of deliverance on the earth.
NET.13: You destroyed the sea by your strength; you shattered the heads of the sea monster in the water.
NET.14: You crushed the heads of Leviathan; you fed him to the people who live along the coast.
NET.15: You broke open the spring and the stream; you dried up perpetually flowing rivers.
NET.16: You established the cycle of day and night; you put the moon and sun in place.
NET.17: You set up all the boundaries of the earth; you created the cycle of summer and winter.
NET.18: Remember how the enemy hurls insults, O LORD, and how a foolish nation blasphemes your name!
NET.19: Do not hand the life of your dove over to a wild animal! Do not continue to disregard the lives of your oppressed people!
NET.20: Remember your covenant promises, for the dark regions of the earth are full of places where violence rules.
NET.21: Do not let the afflicted be turned back in shame! Let the oppressed and poor praise your name!
NET.22: Rise up, O God! Defend your honor! Remember how fools insult you all day long!
NET.23: Do not disregard what your enemies say, or the unceasing shouts of those who defy you.
